question: After editing the **Webhook ID** and authorization fields of an HTTP Webhook connector in Web Modeler, what action makes the new configuration take effect on the public endpoint?

options.text: Save the BPMN file; changes apply automatically without redeployment.
Trigger any process instance; the connector reloads its configuration on each invocation.

a.text: The HTTP Webhook docs state plainly: "If you make changes to your HTTP Webhook connector configuration, you need to redeploy the BPMN diagram for the changes to take effect."

b.text: Saving the BPMN file in Modeler does not update the deployed webhook. The connector binding is tied to the deployment, so a new deploy is required.

c.text: Restarting the connectors runtime does not pick up edits that have not been deployed; the runtime serves the last deployed configuration.

d.text: Triggering an instance executes the currently deployed webhook configuration; it does not reload edits from Modeler.

explanation: Once an HTTP Webhook is deployed, it is reachable at `http(s)://<base URL>/inbound/<webhook ID>` (or `http(s)://<base URL>/<contextPath>/inbound/<webhook ID>` in Self-Managed). Any subsequent change to the connector's properties — including the **Webhook ID** path — only takes effect when the BPMN diagram is redeployed.
